content.problemStatement:
You are given an N x M binary matrix grid, where 0 represents a sea cell and 1
    represents a land cell. A move consists of walking from one land cell to another adjacent (4-directionally) land
    cell or walking off the boundary of the grid. Find the number of land cells in the grid for which we cannot walk off
    the boundary of the grid in any number of moves..
content.inputFormat: A 2D binary matrix grid where 0 represents a sea cell and 1 represents a land cell.
content.outputFormat: The number of land cells in the grid for which we cannot walk off the boundary of the grid in any number of moves.

solution.approach: Use BFS to mark all land cells reachable from the boundary, then count the remaining land cells.
